source_quote: Charging LEDs do not illuminate when 24 V DC adapter is connected; OLED shows zero charge current and wildly incorrect per-cell voltages (e.g., 5.3 V on individual cells); total pack voltage stalls at 3.3-3.5 V after hours on the charger. Likely cause: Cells below the LTC4020 charge-initiation threshold after deep discharge; charger refuses to start sequence

evidence.reasoning: LTC4020 alias maps to U2 per functional candidate map (LTC4020 -> U2).
Charger producing no output is modeled as 'dead' regulator.

evidence.source_quote_substring: total pack voltage stalls at 3.3-3.5 V
evidence.reasoning: U2 is source_refdes for VOUT and INTVCC per graph; pack voltage stall consistent with VOUT offline.

source_quote: OLED shows zero charge current and wildly incorrect per-cell voltages (e.g., 5.3 V on individual cells); LTC6803-4 cell monitor returns nonsense readings when cells are in over-discharge protection. Rail / test point: VREG at U4

evidence.reasoning: LTC6803-4/cell monitor alias maps to U4 per functional candidate map.
Total non-functional reporting of cell voltages indicates dead device.
U4 is source_refdes for VREG per graph; with U4 dead, VREG collapses.

source_quote: Cell reports reverse voltage or deep under-voltage; system shuts down mid-use with an audible click; pack won't sustain load. Likely cause: One or more cells driven to reverse polarity during over-discharge; cell protection FET (Q5, PMV50EPEAR) opens the BAT bus.

evidence.reasoning: Q5 cell protection FET alias maps to refdes Q5 per functional candidate map.
Protection FET in latched-open state is an 'open' failure mode.

source_quote: R-2 motherboard battery cells diverge significantly in voltage during charge cycle; balancing is ineffective. Components mentioned: Q6-Q12 (PMV50EPEAR, cell balancer FETs), U4 (LTC6803-4)

evidence.reasoning: Q6 cell balancer FET alias maps to Q6 per functional candidate map.
Open balancer FET cannot bypass cell, consistent with ineffective balancing.

source_quote: compute module LCD and CPU serial produce no output. Likely cause: Power sequencing fails to sustain main compute rails after load-switch enable; possible overcurrent protection firing on +5 V, +3.3 V, or PCIE1_PWR at the point the compute module is turned on. Components mentioned: U7 (LM2677SX-5, 5 V buck)

evidence.reasoning: U7 5V buck directly named, maps to U7.

evidence.source_quote_substring: Power sequencing fails to sustain main compute rails
evidence.reasoning: Buck unable to sustain 5V rail modeled as dead.
U7 is source_refdes for +5V rail per graph.

source_quote: After sending 1P watch for rail collapse: if PCIE1_PWR collapses immediately, short-hunt that net to ground in diode mode before re-powering. Check U20 AP22815 enable line and output pad for leakage.

evidence.reasoning: U20 AP22815 directly named, maps to U20.
Output pad leakage to GND on the load switch maps to shorted output.
U20 is source_refdes of PCIE1_PWR per graph; short pulls rail down.

source_quote: If absent, trace back through FB15 to U24 AP22815 output - check enable line continuity. EDP_BL_VCC at J24 eDP connector - Internal eDP display does not turn on after chassis reassembly; backlight flickers briefly on occasion then goes dark.

evidence.reasoning: FB15 named directly, maps to refdes FB15.
Open ferrite bead breaks the EDP_BL_VCC path.

evidence.source_quote_substring: Internal eDP display does not turn on
evidence.reasoning: FB15 is source_refdes for EDP_BL_VCC per graph; an open FB15 leaves backlight rail dead.

source_quote: Internal eDP display does not turn on; Components mentioned: U10 (SN65DSI86IPAPQ1, DSI-to-eDP bridge). Probe VCC (1.2 V) and VCCIO (1.8 V) at U10 supply pins for DSI-bridge health.

evidence.reasoning: U10 DSI-to-eDP bridge named, maps to U10.

evidence.source_quote_substring: Internal eDP display does not turn on
evidence.reasoning: Bridge IC dead means no DSI->eDP conversion.

source_quote: Probe VCCIO (1.8 V) at U10 from U17. Internal eDP display does not turn on after chassis reassembly; external HDMI output works normally.

evidence.reasoning: U17 (TLV1117-18 VCCIO regulator) directly named; maps to U17.

evidence.source_quote_substring: Internal eDP display does not turn on
evidence.reasoning: LDO dead -> VCCIO absent -> bridge cannot bring up display.

evidence.source_quote_substring: VCCIO (1.8 V) at U10 from U17
evidence.reasoning: U17 is source_refdes for VCCIO per graph; dead U17 collapses VCCIO.

source_quote: No audio output; kernel log repeats `wm8960 2-001a: Failed to issue reset`; codec cannot be initialized on the I2C bus. Likely cause: WM8960 (U3) fails to acknowledge on I2C; cause may be a cold joint under the QFN body or collapsed DCVDD/AVDD supply rails preventing internal reset.

evidence.reasoning: WM8960/U3 named directly, maps to U3.
Codec non-responsive on I2C is dead.

source_quote: Probe DCVDD and AVDD at U3 supply pins (DC-voltage mode) - both must be present before I2C reset can succeed. DCVDD at U3 pin (from FB18, ~1.8 V); collapsed DCVDD/AVDD supply rails preventing internal reset

evidence.reasoning: FB18 directly named; maps to FB18.
Open FB18 collapses DCVDD supply at U3.
FB18 is source_refdes for DCVDD per graph; open FB18 -> DCVDD dead.

source_quote: AVDD at U3 (from FB21, ~3.3 V); collapsed DCVDD/AVDD supply rails preventing internal reset; both must be present before I2C reset can succeed.

evidence.reasoning: FB21 directly named; maps to FB21.
Open FB21 collapses AVDD at U3.
FB21 is source_refdes for AVDD per graph; open FB21 -> AVDD dead.

source_quote: DBVDD at U3 (from FB20, ~3.3 V); No audio output; kernel log repeats `wm8960 2-001a: Failed to issue reset`; codec cannot be initialized on the I2C bus.

evidence.reasoning: FB20 directly named; maps to FB20.
Open FB20 ferrite breaks DBVDD path.
FB20 is source_refdes for DBVDD per graph; without DBVDD codec is uninitialized.

source_quote: One or more USB-A host ports deliver no power and no data; only partial/erratic enumeration on one port. Likely cause: AP22815 load switch (U24 or U27) not asserting USB_PWR. Probe USB_PWR at U24 output - if absent with LPC powered, check AP22815 enable line for correct logic level.

evidence.reasoning: U24 USB_PWR load switch named; maps to U24 per functional candidate map.
Load switch not asserting output is dead.

evidence.source_quote_substring: USB-A host ports deliver no power
evidence.reasoning: U24 is source_refdes for USB_PWR per graph; dead U24 -> USB_PWR offline -> ports lose power.

source_quote: If USB_PWR is present but ports are dead, probe USBH_3V3 and USBH_1V1 at U9 supply pins; missing 1V1 core points to U9 (QFP-48) needing replacement. TUSB8040PFP USB hub (U9) internal fault locking downstream ports.

evidence.reasoning: U9 TUSB8040PFP named directly; maps to U9.
Hub IC internal fault is dead.

source_quote: AP22815 load switch (U24 or U27) not asserting USB_PWR; or TUSB8040PFP USB hub (U9) internal fault locking downstream ports. Components mentioned: U27 (AP22815, 3V3_PWR_AUX load switch)

evidence.reasoning: U27 named directly; maps to U27.
Load switch not asserting output is dead.
U27 is source_refdes for 3V3_PWR_AUX per graph; dead U27 collapses that rail.
